content: Michigan winters are hard on batteries. A battery that cranked fine in October can leave you stranded in January. Swing by Hayes Auto Works for a free charge-and-load test before the first cold snap.
excerpt: Cold weather drains car batteries fast. Here is how to stay ahead of it this winter.
title: Winter Is Coming: Cold-Weather Battery Tips

content: Squealing, grinding, a soft pedal, or pulling to one side when you stop are all signs your brakes need attention. Sam and the team can inspect pads, rotors, and lines and get you back on the road safely.
excerpt: Squealing or grinding when you brake? Here are the warning signs worth a quick inspection.
title: Brake Warning Signs You Should Never Ignore

content: Clean oil keeps your engine cool and protected. Skipping changes leads to sludge and expensive repairs down the road. We recommend a check every 5,000 miles for most vehicles around Dearborn.
excerpt: Three reasons routine oil changes save you money over the life of your car.
title: Why Regular Oil Changes Matter

content: The Chevelle project is moving along. This month we finished the floor pans and started on the brake lines. Stop in and take a look next time you are at the shop.
excerpt: A look at where David's 1972 Chevelle restoration project stands this month.
title: 1972 Chevelle Restoration: Progress Update

content: Rotating your tires every 5,000 to 7,000 miles keeps wear even and extends tire life. It is a quick job and we usually do it right alongside an oil change.
excerpt: Why rotating your tires on schedule helps them last longer and ride smoother.
title: Tire Rotation: Small Job, Big Difference

content: A check engine light does not always mean disaster, but it should never be ignored. Bring it in and Nadia can pull the codes and explain exactly what your car is telling you.
excerpt: What that check engine light really means and when to bring your car in.
title: Check Engine Light Basics
